pattern: Because only ten LEDs exist, you can enumerate every hour-minute pair and keep those whose total number of set bits equals the requested count, formatting each survivor as a time string.
hints: The watch shows an hour from 0 to 11 and a minute from 0 to 59; the lit LEDs are exactly the set bits of those two numbers combined.
Loop over all valid hour and minute combinations and keep a pair only when the count of one-bits in the hour plus the one-bits in the minute equals the target.
Format each kept pair as hour, a colon, then the minute padded to two digits, and collect them; order does not matter.

edge_inputs.description: one LED lit yields all single-bit hours and minutes
zero LEDs lit is only midnight
nine LEDs cannot be split across a valid hour and minute, so no times
ten LEDs is impossible and gives an empty list
mid count exercising many bit splits
eight LEDs is near the impossible upper edge

stress.description: every legal count of lit LEDs
stress.note: domain is tiny and fully enumerable; covers impossible and feasible counts
